name: Christiana Clark
researcher_question: Did Christiana Clark (nee Schreck, b.1783), widow of William Clark of Coshocton County, Ohio, migrate from Ohio to Wisconsin?
notes: Migration IS real (confirmed via Find a Grave memorial 154768466): Christiana died 1848 in Monroe, Green County, Wisconsin, but was buried back in the family plot at West Carlisle Cemetery, Coshocton, Ohio (a 'died away, buried home' case) -- so the Ohio burial is a genuine confounder, NOT a contradiction. FS-reachable migration evidence (tree-blocked): BLM Land Tract Book entry 'Christiana Clark', Wisconsin, 1847 (sparse -- image_transcribe can pull the land description/county); son Joseph Clark's Wisconsin marriage 1854; sons Joseph (1850) and William (1849) enumerated in Green County, WI census. Husband William Clark predeceased her (estate probated Coshocton, OH), so she migrated as a widow. Difficulty hard: the kept Ohio anchors (all residences 1820-1840 OH, deeds, probate, Ohio burial/Find a Grave) pull toward 'stayed in Ohio'; the agent must correlate the sparse WI land record + sons' WI records to establish migration. Required: she migrated to / was present in Wisconsin. Bonus: exact death place Monroe, Green County (that specific place is on the off-FS Find a Grave page + tree, not the FS index).
